purpose: Define the typed ontology and formal semantic objects required by later universal-proof workstreams without proving RCCD or defining the target class through RCCD vocabulary.
well_formedness: identifiers are globally unique across identity-bearing objects within a system
history is stored in nondecreasing time order
dependency endpoints reference commitments or reasoning facts
a recovered witness has an output and a named procedure
unknown recovery is not converted into recovered or not_recovered
executed transitions and rejected proposals are not conflated
semantic_separations: object identity is distinct from content equality
state is distinct from observation of state
transition occurrence is distinct from transition admissibility
dependency is distinct from mere temporal succession
history event is distinct from an enlarged present-state encoding
representation is distinct from recovery procedure
recovery failure is distinct from evidential unknown
normative classification is distinct from empirical occurrence

foundation_neutrality.reason: UPP-W1 supplies common typed objects only; later workstreams must justify class, contract, representations, and theorems independently.

prohibited_inferences: existence of a typed Commitment object proves recoverable-commitment necessity
existence of TransitionStatus proves constrained-evolution necessity
existence of Dependency proves dependency-sensitive-revision necessity
existence of HistoryEvent proves historical-identity necessity
existence of RecoveryWitness proves uniform-effective-recovery necessity
